results.description:
About Castelion

Castelion is bringing a new approach to defense development and production: one that focuses on short, iterative design cycles, rapid testing in development, and modern commercial manufacturing strategies for production at scale. We design, build, and test next generation weapon systems to give America and its Allies a definitive edge in defense capabilities.

Automation and Controls Engineer

The Automation and Controls Engineer will design, build, and commission robust manufacturing systems for production of hypersonic-flight components.

Success requires a practical application of engineering principles to design, build, test, and commission process hardware, integrate new technologies into our production line, and integrate process equipment and technologies within our production line. We are seeking a hands-on problem solver with strong expertise in electro-mechanical system design and automation systems, as well as experience working with machinery and production environments.

The key to excelling in this position is the effective application of mechanical, electrical, and control systems expertise to enable our manufacturing processes.

Responsibilities

  • Design, build, customize, and test hardware and control systems for integration into composite manufacturing equipment balancing process requirements, cost and lead-time considerations.
  • Partner with mechanical, materials, and manufacturing engineering teams to align on hardware requirements and design solutions.
  • Mature manufacturing processes via the implementation of process control systems and procedures to ensure production consistency and reliability.
  • Work with suppliers and vendors to define specifications and ensure on-time delivery of equipment for production needs.
  • Troubleshoot hardware malfunction issues to minimize downtime and improve operations.
  • Provide guidance and implement robust plans for hardware maintenance and calibration to ensure reliable operation of the manufacturing equipment.

Basic Qualifications

  • Bachelors degree in mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, aerospace engineering or industrial engineering.
  • 2+ years of experience in a fast-paced aerospace, automotive, or manufacturing production environment.
  • Experience identifying, trouble shooting and rectifying complex manufacturing issues involving multiple production resources in a deadline driven environment.
  • Understanding of manufacturing processes (machining, welding, forming, composites).
  • Strong mechanical aptitude and willingness to engage in hands-on work to test and implement ideas.
  • CAD / CAM (Siemens NX).
  • Computer Programming (Python, C, NodeJS, HTML, CSS).

Preferred Skills & Experience

  • Masters degree in mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, aerospace engineering or industrial engineering.
  • 4+ years of experience in a fast-paced aerospace manufacturing and production environment.
  • Automation (PLC programming, Industrial Robotics).
  • CNC Machining.
  • IoT / Connected factory concepts.
  • Networking skills.

Leadership Qualities

  • Bias to Action and Creative Problem Solving. Desire and experience questioning assumptions in ways that lead to break through ideas that are ultimately implemented. Successfully bring in applicable processes/concepts/materials from other industries to achieve efficiency gains. Ability to work independently and resolve minor issues in development with minimal oversight and support.
  • High Commitment, High Initiative. A successful candidate will have a genuine passion for Castelion's mission and consistently look for ways to contribute to the company's technical goals and prevent hardware blockers. Ability to work in a fast paced, autonomously driven, and demanding atmosphere. Strong sense of accountability and integrity.
  • Clear Communicator. Proactively communicates blockers. Trusted in previous roles to be voice of company with regulators, suppliers, gate keepers and customers. Capable of tactfully managing relationships with stakeholders to achieve company-desired outcomes without compromising relationships. Emails, IMs and verbal interactions are logical, drive clarity, and detailed enough to eliminate ambiguity.

ITAR Requirements

  • To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Employment with Castelion is governed on the basis of competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

All employees are granted long-term stock incentives as part of their employment at Castelion. All employees receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental insurance, and the company offers four weeks of paid time off per year.

About Castelion

Castelion is bringing a new approach to defense development and production: one that focuses on short, iterative design cycles, rapid testing in development, and modern commercial manufacturing strategies for production at scale. We're designing, building, and testing next generation long range strike weapons systems to give America and its Allies a definitive edge and deter future conflicts.

Avionics/Electrical Quality Inspector

Castelion is seeking an Avionics / Electrical Quality Inspector - reporting to the Quality organization—to support the development and manufacturing of avionics, harnessing, and electrical assemblies for long-range hypersonic strike vehicles. This is an hourly, non-exempt position located in our Torrance facility. In this role, you will work closely with manufacturing and engineering to ensure avionics and electrical hardware meets all design, workmanship, and compliance requirements prior to flight, test, and delivery.

Responsibilities

  • Perform receiving, in-process, and final inspection of avionics, wire harnesses, electro-mechanical assemblies, cable assemblies, and electrical components
  • Inspect hardware to IPC-A-610, IPC/WHMA-A-620, engineering drawings, and internal workmanship standards
  • Verify electrical assemblies for proper routing, terminations, pinouts, labeling, and configuration
  • Review and validate certifications, traceability, and lot control documentation for electrical materials and components
  • Identify, document, and clearly communicate nonconformances to manufacturing and engineering
  • Partner with engineering to interpret drawings, resolve inspection issues, and support rapid iteration
  • Support first article inspections (FAI) and configuration verification of avionics hardware
  • Maintain accurate inspection records and ensure compliance with quality management system requirements
  • Assist in the development and refinement of inspection procedures and acceptance criteria
  • Support audits and customer or government source inspections as required

Basic Qualifications

  • 3+ years of experience inspecting avionics or electrical assemblies in an aerospace or defense environment
  • Strong ability to read and interpret wiring diagrams, harness drawings, and electrical schematics
  • Working knowledge of IPC-A-610 and IPC/WHMA-A-620 standards
  • Experience using inspection and test equipment such as multimeters and continuity testers
  • Familiarity with electrical workmanship standards and flight hardware requirements
  • High attention to detail with the ability to detect subtle workmanship defects

Preferred Skills and Experience

  • Experience supporting R&D and early production manufacturing environments
  • Prior experience inspecting flight or mission-critical hardware
  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, high-output environment
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to collaborate effectively with manufacturing, engineering, and quality teams
  • Comfortable managing multiple priorities and shifting production schedules
  • Familiarity with digital documentation systems and quality records

All employees are granted long-term stock incentives as part of their employment as Castelion. All employees receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental insurance, and the company offers three weeks of paid time off per year.

Leadership Qualities

Bias to Action and Creative Problem Solving. Desire and experience questioning assumptions in ways that lead to break through ideas that are ultimately implemented. Successfully bring in applicable processes/concepts/materials from other industries to achieve efficiency gains. Ability to personally resolve minor issues in development without requiring significant support.

High Commitment, High Initiative. A successful candidate will have a genuine passion for Castelion's mission and consistently look for ways to contribute to the company's technical goals and prevent hardware blockers. Ability to work in a fast paced, autonomously driven, and demanding atmosphere. Strong sense of accountability and integrity.

Clear Communicator. Proactively communicates blockers. Trusted in previous roles to be voice of company with regulators, suppliers, gate keepers and customers. Capable of tactfully managing relationships with stakeholders to achieve company-desired outcomes without compromising relationships. Emails, IMs and verbal interactions are logical, drive clarity, and detailed enough to eliminate ambiguity.

ITAR Requirements:

  • To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Employment with Castelion is governed on the basis of competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

About Castelion

Castelion is bringing a new approach to defense development and production: one that focuses on short, iterative design cycles, rapid testing in development, and modern commercial manufacturing strategies for production at scale. We're designing, building, and testing next generation long range strike weapons systems to give America and its Allies a definitive edge and deter future conflicts.

Avionics Manufacturing Specialist (PCBA)

Castelion is looking for an Avionics Manufacturing Specialist, reporting to the Lead Manufacturing Engineer of Avionics, to support the development and production of systems required for long-range hypersonic strike vehicles. This will be a full-time, exempt position located in our Torrance, California facility.

As an Avionics Manufacturing Specialist, you will work closely with engineering and manufacturing teams to execute highly technical electrical builds, while owning critical aspects of tooling, process development, work instruction creation, and test infrastructure.

Responsibilities

  • Work with avionics engineering by rapidly iterating electronic components and cables
  • Construct wire harnesses, perform PCBA rework, assemble and test racks/systems, electro-mechanical units, and test equipment
  • Own the development, maintenance, and continuous improvement of manufacturing tooling
  • Create, document, and maintain detailed work instructions to ensure repeatable, high-quality builds
  • Drive process improvements focused on efficiency, repeatability, and first-pass yield
  • Own portions of test infrastructure, including setup, documentation, and continuous improvement of test processes and equipment
  • Create and document testing systems and cable diagrams according to engineer-selected testing gear
  • Collaborate closely with engineering to transform concepts into manufacturable solutions
  • Monitor work progress against standards and deliver on schedule
  • Interpret and execute work from drawings, procedures, and regulated documentation
  • Understand complex cable harnesses, wiring diagrams, and GD&T
  • Verify flight hardware and document results
  • Support transition from development builds into initial production
  • Contribute to initiatives that improve operational efficiency

Basic Qualifications

  • 5+ years of avionics technician or manufacturing experience
  • Experience reading PCBA schematics and executing rework
  • Capability to rework 0201 SMD and BGA components in tight spaces
  • Experience reading wiring harness drawings and building flight or lab hardware
  • Knowledge of industry standards such as IPC-610 and IPC-620
  • Proficient with test equipment such as multimeters, oscilloscopes, and signal generators
  • Experience creating clear, effective work instructions for manufacturing or assembly processes
  • Experience improving manufacturing or assembly processes (yield, efficiency, or repeatability)
  • Experience owning or supporting electrical or avionics test processes and infrastructure
  • Demonstrated direct or indirect leadership

Preferred Skills and Experience

  • Experience in a research and development environment
  • Previous involvement in a high-speed production environment, handling flight hardware
  • Prior involvement in a high-output environment, involving hands-on experience in constructing cables, electro-mechanical devices, or building prototypes
  • Ability to acclimate to frequently changing assignments and a fast-paced working environment
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and oral, along with effective teamwork abilities
  • Superb focus and attention to detail, with remarkable work efficiency and accuracy
  • Experience with soldering PCBAs, including soldering in tight spaces such as 0402 packages and high-density ICs, installing jumpers, and performing dead-bugging
  • Expertise in manufacturing and repairing intricate testing systems
  • Familiarity with Atlassian tools such as SVN, Confluence, and JIRA
  • Basic comprehension of electronic principles and theory
  • Skills in project management

Additional Requirements

  • Must be willing to work extended hours and/or weekends as needed
  • Must be able to travel up to 30% of the time

Leadership Qualities

  • Bias to Action and Creative Problem Solving. Desire and experience questioning assumptions in ways that lead to break through ideas that are ultimately implemented. Successfully bring in applicable processes/concepts/materials from other industries to achieve efficiency gains. Ability to personally resolve minor issues in development without requiring significant support.
  • High Commitment, High Initiative. A successful candidate will have a genuine passion for Castelion's mission and consistently look for ways to contribute to the company's technical goals and prevent hardware blockers. Ability to work in a fast paced, autonomously driven, and demanding atmosphere. Strong sense of accountability and integrity.
  • Clear Communicator.Proactively communicates blockers. Trusted in previous roles to be voice of company with regulators, suppliers, gate keepers and customers. Capable of tactfully managing relationships with stakeholders to achieve company-desired outcomes without compromising relationships. Emails, IMs and verbal interactions are logical, drive clarity, and detailed enough to eliminate ambiguity.

ITAR Requirements

  • To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Employment with Castelion is governed on the basis of competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

All employees are granted long-term stock incentives as part of their employment as Castelion. All employees receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental insurance, and the company offers four weeks of paid time off per year.

About Castelion

Castelion is bringing a new approach to defense development and production: one that focuses on short, iterative design cycles, rapid testing in development, and modern commercial manufacturing strategies for production at scale. We're designing, building, and testing next generation long range strike weapons systems to give America and its Allies a definitive edge and deter future conflicts.

Avionics Integration Technician

Castelion is looking for an Avionics Integration Technician, reporting to the Manager, R&D Operations, to support the development and production of systems required for long-range hypersonic strike vehicles. This will be a full-time, non-exempt position located in our Torrance, California facility.

As an Avionics Integration Technician, you will work closely with engineering and manufacturing teams to assemble, integrate, test, and troubleshoot avionics hardware and electrical systems in support of development and production activities. This role is focused on hands-on vehicle and subsystem integration while supporting rapid development efforts and maintaining high standards of quality and workmanship.

Responsibilities

  • ﻿Integrate avionics hardware, wire harnesses, electrical subsystems, and electro-mechanical assemblies into vehicle and test configurations
  • Perform electrical troubleshooting, continuity verification, and functional testing of avionics systems and support equipment
  • Execute manufacturing and integration processes according to work instructions, drawings, schematics, and engineering documentation
  • Support development builds, prototype integration activities, environmental testing, and transition into initial production
  • Install, route, secure, and terminate wire harnesses and cable assemblies in accordance with engineering requirements and workmanship standards
  • Support HiPot, insulation resistance, and electrical acceptance testing activities as required
  • Read and interpret schematics, wiring diagrams, assembly drawings, and controlled documentation
  • Identify hardware, integration, and test issues and escalate discrepancies to engineering or manufacturing leadership
  • Support process improvement initiatives focused on integration efficiency, quality, and repeatability
  • Maintain a clean, organized, and ESD-compliant work environment
  • Monitor work progress against schedules and support production priorities as needed

Basic Qualifications

  • ﻿2+ years of avionics technician, integration technician, aerospace manufacturing, or related hands-on experience
  • Experience reading electrical schematics, wiring diagrams, and harness drawings
  • Experience with avionics hardware integration, harness routing, and electrical assembly
  • Familiarity with IPC-610 and IPC-620 standards
  • Ability to follow work instructions, engineering drawings, and controlled documentation
  • Strong attention to detail and workmanship quality
  • Experience working in an ESD-controlled manufacturing environment
  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced development and production environment

Preferred Skills and Experience

  • Experience in aerospace, defense, or other highly regulated industries
  • Experience integrating flight hardware, prototype hardware, or test systems
  • Experience troubleshooting electrical or avionics systems at the subsystem or vehicle level
  • Familiarity with manufacturing documentation and configuration-controlled environments
  • Experience supporting engineering development builds and rapid iteration efforts
  • Familiarity with environmental test activities such as vibration, thermal, or vacuum testing
  • Familiarity with Atlassian tools such as JIRA and Confluence
  • Basic understanding of electronic troubleshooting and diagnostics
  • Strong communication and teamwork skills
  • Ability to adapt to changing priorities and evolving hardware designs

Additional Requirements

  • ﻿Must be willing to work extended hours and/or weekends as needed
  • Must be able to travel up to 20% of the time

Leadership Qualities

  • ﻿Bias to Action and Creative Problem Solving. Desire and experience questioning assumptions in ways that lead to break through ideas that are ultimately implemented. Successfully bring in applicable processes/concepts/materials from other industries to achieve efficiency gains. Ability to personally resolve minor issues in development without requiring significant support.
  • High Commitment, High Initiative. A successful candidate will have a genuine passion for Castelion's mission and consistently look for ways to contribute to the company's technical goals and prevent hardware blockers. Ability to work in a fast paced, autonomously driven, and demanding atmosphere. Strong sense of accountability and integrity.
  • Clear Communicator. Proactively communicates blockers. Trusted in previous roles to be voice of company with regulators, suppliers, gate keepers and customers. Capable of tactfully managing relationships with stakeholders to achieve company-desired outcomes without compromising relationships. Emails, IMs and verbal interactions are logical, drive clarity, and detailed enough to eliminate ambiguity.

ITAR Requirements

To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Employment with Castelion is governed on the basis of competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

All employees are granted long-term stock incentives as part of their employment as Castelion. All employees receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental insurance, and the company offers four weeks of paid time off per year.

About Castelion

Castelion is bringing a new approach to defense development and production: one that focuses on short, iterative design cycles, rapid testing in development, and modern commercial manufacturing strategies for production at scale. We're designing, building, and testing next generation long range strike weapons systems to give America and its Allies a definitive edge and deter future conflicts.

Avionics R&D Tech II/Senior (PCBA)

Castelion is looking for an Avionics PCBA Technician, reporting to the Manager, R&D Operations, to support the development and production of systems required for long-range hypersonic strike vehicles. This will be a full-time, non-exempt position located in our Torrance, California facility.

As an Avionics PCBA Technician, you will work closely with engineering and manufacturing teams to execute electrical and electro-mechanical builds in support of development and production activities. This role is focused on hands-on assembly, rework, testing, and hardware integration while supporting manufacturing processes, documentation, and continuous improvement efforts.

Responsibilities

  • Perform PCBA soldering and rework, including installation of jumpers and component replacement as required
  • Build wire harnesses, cable assemblies, PCBAs, electro-mechanical units, and test equipment
  • Assemble, integrate, and test avionics hardware and support equipment
  • Execute manufacturing and test processes according to work instructions, drawings, and engineering documentation
  • Support development builds, prototype builds, and transition into initial production
  • Assist with setup and maintenance of tooling, fixtures, and manufacturing equipment
  • Read and interpret schematics, wiring diagrams, assembly drawings, and controlled documentation
  • Identify build issues and escalate discrepancies to engineering or manufacturing leadership
  • Support process improvement initiatives focused on quality, repeatability, and efficiency
  • Maintain a clean, organized, and ESD-compliant work environment
  • Monitor work progress against schedules and support production priorities as needed

Basic Qualifications

  • 2+ years of avionics technician, electronics manufacturing, or related hands-on assembly experience
  • Experience reading electrical schematics and wiring harness drawings
  • Experience performing soldering and PCBA rework
  • Experience working with IPC-610 and IPC-620 standards
  • Familiarity with avionics, electrical, or electro-mechanical assembly processes
  • Ability to follow work instructions, engineering drawings, and controlled documentation
  • Strong attention to detail and workmanship quality
  • Experience working in an ESD-controlled manufacturing environment
  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced development and production environment

Preferred Skills and Experience

  • Experience in aerospace, defense, or other highly regulated industries
  • Experience performing rework on fine-pitch or high-density PCBAs
  • Experience building flight hardware, prototype hardware, or test systems
  • Familiarity with manufacturing documentation and configuration-controlled environments
  • Experience supporting engineering development builds and rapid iteration efforts
  • Familiarity with Atlassian tools such as JIRA and Confluence
  • Basic understanding of electronic troubleshooting and diagnostics
  • Strong communication and teamwork skills
  • Ability to adapt to changing priorities and evolving hardware designs

Additional Requirements

  • Must be willing to work extended hours and/or weekends as needed
  • Must be able to travel up to 20% of the time

Leadership Qualities

  • Bias to Action and Creative Problem Solving. Desire and experience questioning assumptions in ways that lead to break through ideas that are ultimately implemented. Successfully bring in applicable processes/concepts/materials from other industries to achieve efficiency gains. Ability to personally resolve minor issues in development without requiring significant support.
  • High Commitment, High Initiative. A successful candidate will have a genuine passion for Castelion's mission and consistently look for ways to contribute to the company's technical goals and prevent hardware blockers. Ability to work in a fast paced, autonomously driven, and demanding atmosphere. Strong sense of accountability and integrity.
  • Clear Communicator. Proactively communicates blockers. Trusted in previous roles to be voice of company with regulators, suppliers, gate keepers and customers. Capable of tactfully managing relationships with stakeholders to achieve company-desired outcomes without compromising relationships. Emails, IMs and verbal interactions are logical, drive clarity, and detailed enough to eliminate ambiguity.

ITAR Requirements

  • To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Employment with Castelion is governed on the basis of competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

All employees are granted long-term stock incentives as part of their employment as Castelion. All employees receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental insurance, and the company offers four weeks of paid time off per year.

About Castelion

Castelion is redefining defense development through rapid iteration, aggressive testing, and modern commercial manufacturing at scale. We design, build, and test next-generation long-range strike weapon systems to give the United States and its Allies a decisive advantage and deter future conflicts.

Avionics Reliability Engineer

We are hiring an Avionics Reliability Engineer to own environmental qualification and reliability validation across our avionics hardware. This role is responsible for developing and executing reliability test strategies, including HALT/HASS campaigns, and driving hardware improvement through deep failure analysis and cross-functional collaboration. This position is ideal for engineers who want hands-on ownership of environmental testing infrastructure and reliability processes while working closely with avionics design and manufacturing teams to rapidly mature flight hardware.

Responsibilities

  • Develop reliability validation strategies for avionics hardware, including environmental and stress testing methodologies
  • Design and execute HALT/HASS campaigns to identify failure modes and accelerate hardware maturation
  • Specify, procure, and commission environmental test equipment and chambers
  • Oversee and conduct environmental testing, including vibration, thermal cycling, and stress screening
  • Perform live troubleshooting and root-cause analysis on hardware under test
  • Translate test findings into actionable design improvements and engineering change orders (ECOs)
  • Partner with avionics design engineers to improve robustness and manufacturability
  • Support manufacturing by developing and implementing screening procedures, authoring and verifying acceptance test procedures, and establishing reliability best practices to ensure product compliance and long-term performance.
  • Create and maintain clear documentation of test procedures, results, and reliability metrics
  • Drive continuous improvement of reliability processes and test infrastructure

Basic Qualifications

  • Bachelors degree in electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, or related field
  • Hands-on experience with environmental or reliability testing of electronic hardware
  • Experience troubleshooting complex hardware systems in a lab environment
  • Familiarity with standard lab instrumentation and data acquisition
  • Strong understanding of failure analysis and root-cause methodologies
  • Ability to work across design, test, and manufacturing teams

Preferred Skills and Experience

  • Experience developing or running HALT/HASS or other accelerated life testing programs
  • Familiarity with environmental chambers (thermal, vibration, combined stress)
  • Experience with avionics, aerospace, automotive, or other high-reliability hardware
  • Background in reliability engineering, qualification testing, or validation engineering
  • Experience writing test automation or analysis scripts (Python or similar)
  • Knowledge of industry reliability standards and qualification practices

Leadership Qualities

  • Bias to Action and Creative Problem Solving. Desire and experience questioning assumptions in ways that lead to break through ideas that are ultimately implemented. Successfully bring in applicable processes/concepts/materials from other industries to achieve efficiency gains. Ability to personally resolve minor issues in development without requiring significant support.
  • High Commitment, High Initiative. A successful candidate will have a genuine passion for Castelion's mission and consistently look for ways to contribute to the company's technical goals and prevent hardware blockers. Ability to work in a fast paced, autonomously driven, and demanding atmosphere. Strong sense of accountability and integrity.
  • Clear Communicator. Proactively communicates blockers. Trusted in previous roles to be voice of company with regulators, suppliers, gate keepers and customers. Capable of tactfully managing relationships with stakeholders to achieve company-desired outcomes without compromising relationships. Emails, IMs and verbal interactions are logical, drive clarity, and detailed enough to eliminate ambiguity.

ITAR Requirements

  • To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Employment with Castelion is governed on the basis of competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

All employees are granted long-term stock incentives as part of their employment at Castelion. All employees receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental insurance, and the company offers four weeks of paid time off per year.

About Castelion

Castelion is bringing a new approach to defense development and production: one that focuses on short, iterative design cycles, rapid testing in development, and modern commercial manufacturing strategies for production at scale. We design, build, and test next generation weapon systems to give America and its Allies a definitive edge in defense capabilities.

Build Reliability Engineer

The Build Reliability Engineer will lead initiatives aimed at continuous improvement in the reliability and quality of our products. You will develop and implement internal processes and manufacturing software to optimize development and production workflows and ensure high-quality output. You will guide teams in conducting reliability assessments, implementing preventative measures, and addressing quality issues through root cause analyses and corrective actions, all while ensuring compliance with industry standards and regulatory requirements.

Responsibilities

Design and Manufacturing

  • Work closely with design, manufacturing, and operations teams to ensure reliability and quality considerations are integrated into all phases of the product lifecycle.
  • Participate in Design and Build Reviews to ensure design for manufacturability and inspectability.
  • Reviews and approves Process Plans, specifications, and build documentation for completeness, accuracy, regulatory compliance, and quality objectives.
  • Support the development and implementation of internal processes and manufacturing software to optimize production efficiency, product quality, and change control.
  • Facilitate the engineering change control process (ECR/ECO) with a focus on traceability and impact alignment to maintain clean configuration genealogy.

Inspection and Test Process

  • Ensure appropriate test validation methods and inspection techniques have been considered to validate design and manufacturing processes.
  • Develop and implement Quality Control Processes, and Standards, and Planning, ensuring compliance with internal and external standards and regulations.
  • Design or procures new measurement equipment as required to achieve required inspections with maximum efficiency.
  • Validates measurement and tests methods using process capability and measurement systems analysis (MSA) statistical tools. Propose solutions to remove the need to verify product conformance through measurement, e.g. poka-yoke.

Evaluates and analyzes production quality landscape

  • Utilize reliability and quality data to generate reports, identify trends, and recommend improvements to enhance product and process reliability and performance.
  • Uses data to make landscape transparent and drive project prioritization. Responsible for ensuring that issues related to product reliability are primary focus.
  • Drives achievement of quality targets through a review hierarchy with appropriate levels of management.

Corrective Action Preventive Action

  • Utilize reliability and quality data to generate reports, identify trends, and recommend improvements to enhance product and process reliability and performance.
  • Leverage tools like FMEA and statistical analysis to identify and prevent quality and reliability issues.
  • Leads, facilitates, and participates in root cause investigations and ensure appropriate action to prevent recurrence.

Nonconformance Management

  • Support review of all nonconformance conditions for adequate resolution and rationale prior to closure.
  • Lead containment functions to include impact and risk analysis.
  • Oversee control of all non-conforming hardware

Compliance and Training

  • Support efforts to implement and adhere to Quality Management System policies and other regulatory requirements.
  • Facilitate creation of training plans and materials for technician and inspector competency.
  • Develop training materials and provide support to end-users to ensure effective utilization of manufacturing and management system software.
  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives aimed at enhancing product reliability and risk mitigation. 

Requirements and skills

  • Minimum 4+ years in reliability and/or quality engineering role

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience in aerospace or defense/government programs is highly desirable.
  • Experience with implementation and enhancement of Manufacturing Enterprise Software.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present complex technical information to diverse audiences.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a track record of implementing effective solutions to complex reliability and quality challenges.
  • Experience supporting diverse, high volume, production teams during production ramp-up.
  • Knowledge of quality tools such as Lean principles, Six Sigma, Root Cause Analysis and Corrective Action, Advanced Product Quality Planning (APQP), Metrology, Process Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (PFMEA), Control Plans, and various problem-solving approaches.
  • Dedicated to adopting a Lean approach to quality without compromising safety or reliability.

Leadership Qualities

Bias to Action and Creative Problem Solving. Desire and experience questioning assumptions in ways that lead to break through ideas that are ultimately implemented. Successfully bring in applicable processes/concepts/materials from other industries to achieve efficiency gains. Ability to personally resolve minor issues in development without requiring significant support.

High Commitment, High Initiative. A successful candidate will have a genuine passion for Castelion's mission and consistently look for ways to contribute to the company's technical goals and prevent hardware blockers. Ability to work in a fast paced, autonomously driven, and demanding atmosphere. Strong sense of accountability and integrity.

Clear Communicator. Proactively communicates blockers. Trusted in previous roles to be voice of company with regulators, suppliers, gate keepers and customers. Capable of tactfully managing relationships with stakeholders to achieve company-desired outcomes without compromising relationships. Emails, IMs and verbal interactions are logical, drive clarity, and detailed enough to eliminate ambiguity.

ITAR Requirements

  • To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Employment with Castelion is governed on the basis of competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

Why Castelion, Why Now

Castelion is moving incredibly fast to develop and deliver advanced defense systems at a time when execution matters more than ever. We believe focus and technical excellence are decisive advantages - and we are building a team that can deliver real capability, not just concepts.

This is a rare opportunity to join at an early stage, where your work will directly shape critical systems, influence major technical decisions, and have immediate, real-world impact.

Chemical Technician – Wet Chemistry

Castelion is seeking a highly motivated Chemical Technician to support wet chemistry processing, laboratory operations, and chemical reaction workflows. This role is ideal for an individual with hands-on laboratory experience who is comfortable working with hazardous chemicals and supporting chemical manufacturing or R&D activities in a fast-paced environment.

The role combines laboratory operations, chemical handling, equipment preparation, and process monitoring responsibilities. The technician will support the preparation and maintenance of laboratory glassware, assist with reaction setup and assembly, monitor active chemical processes, and ensure safe and compliant laboratory operations.

Responsibilities

  • Clean, inspect, and prepare laboratory glassware according to established procedures and cleanliness standards.
  • Assist with assembly, setup, and disassembly of glassware and reaction systems.
  • Support wet chemistry processing activities under the direction of engineers and chemists.
  • Collect and record process data, observations, and operating conditions during chemical processing runs.
  • Safely handle, transfer, store, and dispose of hazardous chemicals in accordance with company procedures and regulatory requirements.
  • Prepare chemical solutions, reagents, and process materials as directed.
  • Perform routine laboratory housekeeping and maintain an organized work environment.
  • Assist with troubleshooting process and equipment issues during chemical operations.
  • Support inventory management of chemicals, consumables, and laboratory supplies.
  • Follow all environmental, health, and safety (EHS) requirements and participate in safety training activities.

Basic Qualifications

  • Associate degree, technical certification, or equivalent experience in Chemistry, Chemical Technology, or a related field.
  • Experience working in a laboratory, chemical processing, manufacturing, or industrial environment.
  • Familiarity with safe handling practices for corrosive, toxic, flammable, and reactive chemicals.
  • Experience using laboratory glassware and wet chemistry equipment.
  • Ability to read and follow standard operating procedures (SOPs), safety data sheets (SDS), and work instructions.
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to maintain accurate records.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a hands-on laboratory environment.
  • Ability to lift laboratory equipment and stand for extended periods as required.

Preferred Skills and Experience

  • Experience with chemical reaction systems, pilot-scale processing, or industrial chemical operations.
  • Familiarity with acid/base handling, solvent systems, and chemical waste management.
  • Experience working under OSHA, EPA, or other regulated laboratory environments.
  • Knowledge of process monitoring instrumentation and data collection methods.

Benefits And Perks

  • Employee Equity: Meaningful equity for all full-time employees - share value has increased 150x in three years
  • Personal Time Off: Four (4) weeks of paid time off to unplug, reset, and come back sharp—velocity matters, burnout doesn't
  • Company Paid Holidays: Ten (10) company paid holidays
  • Medical: We offer plans at 100% employee covered and 80% of dependent coverage
  • Dental & Vision: We cover 90% employees and 80% for dependents to ensure you and your family have access to quality preventive and ongoing care
  • FSA/HSA: To support long-term health and financial resilience, we offer FSA and HSA options that allow employees to set aside pre-tax dollars for eligible medical and dependent care expenses
  • Retirement: 401k plan offered to support long term financial growth along side high-impact, high-velocity work
  • Paid Parental Leave: We support growing families with meaningful time to bond and recover. Birthing parents are eligible for up to 10 weeks of paid leave, while non-birthing parents may take up to 4 weeks of paid leave. This time can be used for pre-natal care, pregnancy, childbirth, and related medical needs—ensuring every parent has the support they need during this important life moment.8-weeks of paid parental leave for all parents. Additional paid pregnancy leave for CA employees
  • Meals & Catering: Frequently catered lunches and team meals, plus company-covered meals when extra time or effort is required
  • Snacks & Beverages: Fully stocked kitchen with unlimited snacks and drinks to keep you fueled throughout the day
  • Performance & Fitness: $100 monthly fitness stipend to support peak performance and health, both inside and outside of work
  • EV Charging: Onsite electric vehicle charging available for employees

Physical Demands

  • Must be willing to work extended hours and/or weekends as needed
  • Must be able to travel up to 30%
  • Must be able to lift more than 50lbs

ITAR Requirements

To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

This position may require access to classified information or restricted U.S. Government sites, systems, or information, as determined by the Company and/or applicable U.S. Government requirements. If the position is so designated, your employment in the role may be contingent upon your ability to obtain and maintain the required U.S. Government security clearance or other government authorization, and to satisfy any citizenship or other eligibility requirements imposed by applicable law, regulation, executive order, or government contract requirements. You will be notified if and when such requirements apply. Failure to obtain or maintain any required clearance or authorization may result in reassignment, leave, or separation from employment, consistent with business needs and applicable law.

Affirmative Action/EEO Statement

Employment with Castelion is governed on the basis of competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

Other Duties

Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

Why Castelion, Why Now

Castelion is moving incredibly fast to develop and deliver advanced defense systems at a time when execution matters more than ever. We believe focus and technical excellence are decisive advantages - and we are building a team that can deliver real capability, not just concepts.

This is a rare opportunity to join at an early stage, where your work will directly shape critical systems, influence major technical decisions, and have immediate, real-world impact.

CNC Machinist – 5 Axis – 2nd Shift (230pm to 11pm)

As a CNC Machinist at Castelion, you will be responsible for machining & manufacturing complex components for high performance aerospace products.

Responsibilities

  • Set up, operate, and program multi-axis CNC mills and lathes (up to 5-axis) to manufacture complex aerospace components
  • Perform precision inspections using an array of metrology equipment to verify dimensions and ensure adherence to drawings and tolerances
  • Read and interpret blueprints, CAD drawings, GD&T, and other technical documents to complete machining operations
  • Manage cutting tools in CNC machines, ensuring optimal tool load and measurement for upcoming programs
  • Troubleshoot and resolve operational issues, including alarm resolutions and tooling repairs, to maintain maximum machine uptime
  • Operate CNC machines in a production environment, producing both development and flight hardware while meeting quick-turn program goals
  • Conduct on-machine deburring and precision cleaning of components to ensure quality and reduce human intervention
  • Maintain a clean and safe work environment, adhering to company guidelines and 5S principles
  • Provide continuous feedback for process improvements and efficiency opportunities to engineering and leadership
  • Actively participate in preventative maintenance of CNC machines on a weekly and monthly basis
  • Communicate with shop leadership regarding production challenges and collaborate with teammates to support workflow

Basic Qualifications

  • High school diploma or equivalency certificate
  • 5+ years of experience in CNC machining, particularly with aerospace machine shops and hard metals
  • Demonstrated proficiency in G-code, with strong setup and operational skills on CNC mills and lathes
  • Ability to read and understand complex blueprints, GD&T, and tolerances

Preferred Skills and Experience

  • Experience with various materials such as Inconel, titanium, stainless steel, copper, and aluminum
  • 3+ years of setup experience on vertical mills
  • 1+ year of experience setting up and operating 5-axis mills
  • Familiarity with CNC programming using software such as NX, Solidworks HSM, Mastercam, Fusion 360 or Esprit CAD/CAM
  • Experience with multiple CNC control systems, including Heidenhain, Haas, Fanuc, Siemens, and others
  • Strong mechanical aptitude and understanding of tooling technology and shop math
  • Excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to contribute to a team-oriented environment.

Benefits And Perks

  • Employee Equity: Meaningful equity for all full-time employees - share value has increased 150x in three years
  • Personal Time Off: Four (4) weeks of paid time off to unplug, reset, and come back sharp—velocity matters, burnout doesn't

  • FSA/HSA: To support long-term health and financial resilience, we offer FSA and HSA options that allow employees to set aside pre-tax dollars for eligible medical and dependent care expenses
  • Retirement: 401k plan offered to support long term financial growth along side high-impact, high-velocity work
  • Paid Parental Leave: We support growing families with meaningful time to bond and recover. results.position_name: Composites R&D Technician – Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD)
results.description:
About Castelion

Castelion is bringing a new approach to defense development and production: one that focuses on short, iterative design cycles, rapid testing in development, and modern commercial manufacturing strategies for production at scale. We're designing, building, and testing next generation long range strike weapons systems to give America and its Allies a definitive edge and deter future conflicts.

Composites R&D Technician – Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD)

Castelion is hiring a R&D Technician to support its ceramic matrix composite (CMC) pilot line, focused on operating chemical vapor deposition (CVD) reactors. This is a hands-on role involving equipment operation, process monitoring, and consistent execution of reactor runs.

Responsibilities include setting up and running cycles, recording process data, and identifying and troubleshooting issues as they arise. Strong attention to detail, adherence to safety protocols, and disciplined documentation are essential.

The ideal candidate is comfortable working with complex equipment and control systems, and brings a practical, problem-solving mindset. This role works closely with engineering teams to support process development and improvement.

Responsibilities

  • Supporting of ceramic matrix composite pilot line build and operation
  • Monitor, operate and troubleshoot chemical vapor deposition (CVD) reactors
  • Industrial equipment operation and maintenance
  • Assist process development of chemical vapor deposition process
  • Setting up and running cycles for material batches
  • Recording process data and notating any specific production anomalies for further investigation
  • Trouble shooting pilot line mechanical and electrical issues
  • Enforce safety protocols for furnace and heavy machinery operation

Basic Qualifications

  • High school diploma
  • 2+ years of experience in hands on mechanical, aerospace or manufacturing environment

Preferred Skills and Experience

  • Must be willing to swing shift (3:30pm to 11:30pm)
  • Experience operating and serving complex machinery including chemical reactors, high-temperature furnaces, automated composite layup equipment, or petroleum hardware
  • 2 years of experience with aerospace composites
  • 3-years of experience in the aerospace industry
  • Experience with analog and digital instrumentation (control systems, load cells, pressure transducers, strain gauges, encoders, deflection gauges, and accelerometers)

Additional Requirements

  • Must be willing to work extended hours and/or weekends as needed
  • Must be able to travel up to 20% of the time

Leadership Qualities

  • Bias to Action and Creative Problem Solving. Desire and experience questioning assumptions in ways that lead to break through ideas that are ultimately implemented. Successfully bring in applicable processes/concepts/materials from other industries to achieve efficiency gains. Ability to personally resolve minor issues in development without requiring significant support.
  • High Commitment, High Initiative. A successful candidate will have a genuine passion for Castelion's mission and consistently look for ways to contribute to the company's technical goals and prevent hardware blockers. Ability to work in a fast paced, autonomously driven, and demanding atmosphere. Strong sense of accountability and integrity.
  • Clear Communicator. Proactively communicates blockers. Trusted in previous roles to be voice of company with regulators, suppliers, gate keepers and customers. Capable of tactfully managing relationships with stakeholders to achieve company-desired outcomes without compromising relationships. Emails, IMs and verbal interactions are logical, drive clarity, and detailed enough to eliminate ambiguity.

ITAR Requirements

  • To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Employment with Castelion is governed on the basis of competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

All employees are granted long-term stock incentives as part of their employment at Castelion. All employees receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental insurance, and the company offers four weeks of paid time off per year.
